discussion.p: added tuple protocols, i.e., `tuple_size`, `tuple_element`, and `get` specializations for `integer_sequence`, exactly the same thing we did for `complex` in .
However, unlike the latter, the former lacks an extension to the tuple-like concept, which makes it unable to use any tuple-based range algorithms in the standard.
This feels wrong to me. If `integer_sequence` now walks like a tuple, and quacks like a tuple, then it should be tuple-like.

blockquote.pre: template<class T> concept tuple-like = see below; // exposition only
blockquote.p: -1- A type `T` models and satisfies the exposition-only concept tuple-like if remove_cvref_t<T> is a specialization of `array`, `complex`, `integer_sequence`, `pair`, `tuple`, or `ranges::subrange`.
